message.content:
Research the HISTORY, MILESTONES and LISTING of Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd, an Indian Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS) company in Mysore (NSE: VINNY).

Use WebSearch then WebFetch on authoritative sources (vinyasit.com, screener.in, BSE/NSE, Economic Times, Business Standard, moneycontrol, chittorgarh, IPO sites).

Gather:
1. Founding year / incorporation year.
2. IPO / listing date and which exchange (NSE Emerge SME vs main board).
3. Any migration from SME platform to NSE/BSE main board, and date.
4. Other key historical milestones.

You have WebSearch and WebFetch tools. Run several searches and fetch the most authoritative pages.

Return a concise markdown bullet list of findings. After EACH fact put the exact source URL in parentheses. Distinguish confirmed facts from estimates. Flag anything unverified. Do NOT write files — return findings as your text output.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the web search results, here's the information about Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd:

## Founding Year and Incorporation

Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited was founded in 2001 and is headquartered in Mysore. The company was founded by N. Narendra and a team of technocrats.

## Company Overview

Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited is a prominent Indian Electronic Manufacturing Services (EMS) provider. It specializes in design, engineering, and PCB assembly for global OEMs in the aerospace, defense, automotive, and medical sectors.

## Development History

The company's growth can be divided into phases:

- **Phase 1 (2001-2010)**: During this period, the company initially focused on simple electronic assemblies, with focus on stabilizing quality standards and building a reputation within the local Mysore and Bangalore industrial hubs.

- **Phase 2 (2011-2018)**: Recognizing the limitations of general EMS, Vinyas shifted its focus toward high-reliability sectors and secured AS9100 certification, allowing it to enter the aerospace and defense supply chains.

In FY2025, the company reported strong growth, with annual revenue rising 24.74% to ₹400.15 crore and net profit increasing 26.56% to ₹19.42 crore.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Vinyas Innovative Technologies and its NSE listing:

## Company Overview

Vinyas Innovative Technologies specializes in design, engineering, and electronics manufacturing services, catering to global original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and original design manufacturers (ODMs) in the electronics sector. Its key product is printed circuit boards (PCBs).

## IPO and SME Listing

Vinyas Innovative Technologies IPO, a SME IPO worth ₹51.92 Cr, was launched from September 27, 2023, to October 3, 2023, and got listed on NSE SME on October 6, 2023. The public issue of Vinyas Innovative Technologies IPO (VINYAS) was offered at ₹165.00 per share and was listed at ₹330, delivering a listing gain of 100.00%.

## Migration to Main Board

Regarding migration to the NSE main board, the company's publicly available shareholding patterns indicate a healthy mix of retail and institutional participation, which is a positive sign for a company on the SME exchange looking to eventually migrate to the main board. However, the search results do not contain specific information about an actual migration announcement or timeline for moving from the NSE SME platform to the NSE main board.

content.content:
# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd - Company Information

**Incorporation Year:** 2001

**About the Company:**
"Incorporated in 2001, Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited provides design, engineering, and electronics manufacturing services" for original equipment manufacturers and original design manufacturers in the electronics sector.

**Business Overview:**
The company is ISO 9001:2015, AS 9100-D, and ISO 13485:2016 certified. It specializes in printed circuit boards and delivers comprehensive solutions including "design for manufacturability, supply chain management, PCB assembly and advanced test solutions, product integration, Testing and Box Build Assemblies, and after-market support."

**SME to Main Board Migration:**
No mention of migration from SME platform to NSE or BSE main board is found in the provided content. The company is currently listed on NSE under the SME segment (NSE - SME: VINYAS) and is part of the Nifty SME Emerge index.

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies: Key Information

**Founding/Incorporation Year:**
- Incorporated in 2001

**Historical Milestones:**
- 2000: Company founded by Narendra Narayanan with vision to establish global presence in electronics manufacturing
- 2001: Official incorporation year
- 2011-2012: Received "The Best Employer" award from National Trust, Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India
- 2014: Narendra received Widney House Volunteer Award from University of Southern California
- 2015: Deepashree Narendra joined as Vice President - Corporate Affairs
- 2018: Sumukh Narendra joined as Executive Director

**Certifications & Standards:**
The company follows multiple IPC standards including:
- IPC-J-STD-003B, IPC-CM-770, IPC-HDBK-001, J-STD-001E
- IPC-A-610D (Class 1, 2 & 3)
- IPC-WHMA-A-620A (Class 1, 2 & 3)

Personnel certifications include Six-Sigma, IPC Standards, Joint Industry Standards, and ISO Internal Auditors.

**Stock Exchange/IPO:**
- SME-IPO listed (referenced in navigation menu; specific details not provided in the content)

I found information about Vinyas Innovative Technologies, which is listed on the NSE. The search results show that Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited was incorporated in 2001 and is based in Mysore, India, and it offers Electronic Manufacturing Services (EMS), providing design, engineering, and manufacturing solutions for defense, aerospace, and medical sectors.

However, the search results do not contain specific information about a migration announcement circular from SME platform to the NSE mainboard. The results mention that the National Stock Exchange of India (NSE) has announced revised eligibility criteria for migration from the SME Platform (NSE Emerge) to the Mainboard, which will come into effect from 1 May 2025, but they don't include details about a specific announcement circular for Vinyas Innovative Technologies' migration.

To find the specific announcement circular, you may want to check the NSE website directly or search for recent regulatory filings from Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited.

The page confirms the company's listing as "Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd (VINV)" with NSE designation, and provides current trading data reflecting its main board status.

content.text: I have enough to confirm the migration occurred. The NSE get-quote URL (without "/SM" suffix) and multiple data providers (Groww, investing.com) now show it on the main board, whereas at SME listing it would carry a "-SM" symbol suffix. Let me confirm the migration date with one more targeted search.

input.query: "Vinyas" VINYAS NSE SME platform Emerge migration mainboard effective date 2024 OR 2025 trading

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding SME migration to the mainboard on the NSE:

## Recent SME Migration Examples

Viviana Power Tech secured final NSE approval to migrate from SME EMERGE to NSE Main Board, effective June 2, 2026, enhancing liquidity and investor access.

## Updated Eligibility Criteria (Effective May 1, 2025)

The National Stock Exchange of India (NSE) announced revised eligibility criteria for migration from the SME Platform (NSE Emerge) to the Mainboard, which came into effect from 1 May 2025. The key requirements include:

- Paid-up equity capital must be at least INR 10 crores
- Average market capitalization must be at least INR 100 crores
- Revenue from operations must be greater than INR 100 crores in the most recent financial year
- The company must have reported a positive operating profit (EBITDA) from operations in at least 2 out of the last 3 financial years
- The company must have been listed on the NSE SME platform for a minimum of 3 years
- The number of public shareholders must be at least 500 as of the date of application

## Overall Platform Statistics

As of 28 April 2026, 524 companies are available for trade on NSE Emerge, while 159 companies have been migrated to the Mainboard.

content.text:
I have enough corroborated data. Note there's a slight ambiguity in ticker symbols across providers (VINYAS vs VINV), but the NSE symbol is VINYAS. The migration to main board is confirmed by multiple data providers now classifying it as NSE main board, though I could not pin down the exact migration date. Here are my findings.

## Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd (NSE: VINYAS) — History, Milestones & Listing

### Founding / Incorporation
- **Incorporated in 2001** in Mysore (Mysuru), Karnataka. CONFIRMED across multiple sources ([screener.in](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/); company site [vinyasit.com](https://www.vinyasit.com/company-overview/)).
- Founded by **N. Narendra (Narendra Narayanan)** with a team of technocrats; the vision/founding is dated to 2000 with official incorporation in 2001 ([vinyasit.com company-overview, via fetch](https://www.vinyasit.com/company-overview/)).
- Business: Indian **Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS)** provider — design, engineering, PCB assembly, box-build, test solutions — serving aerospace, defense, medical and industrial OEMs/ODMs ([screener.in](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/)).

### IPO / Listing
- **SME IPO on NSE Emerge (NSE SME platform).** This was an SME-segment listing, not a main-board IPO. CONFIRMED ([ipowatch.in](https://ipowatch.in/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ipo-date-review-price-allotment-details/); [ipopremium.in](https://www.ipopremium.in/view/ipo/282)).
- **IPO open:** September 27, 2023 — **close:** October 3, 2023 ([ipowatch.in](https://ipowatch.in/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ipo-date-review-price-allotment-details/)).
- **Listing date:** **October 6, 2023** on NSE SME. CONFIRMED by majority of sources. (One source cited an earlier tentative date of 11 October 2023; this appears to be a pre-listing estimate and is superseded.) ([ipowatch.in](https://ipowatch.in/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ipo-date-review-price-allotment-details/); [investorgain.com](https://www.investorgain.com/ipo/vinyas-innovative-technologies-sme-ipo/651/)).
- **Issue price band:** ₹162–₹165 per share; **issue size ~₹54.66 crore** (3,312,800 equity shares, face value ₹10). CONFIRMED ([ipowatch.in](https://ipowatch.in/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ipo-date-review-price-allotment-details/)). (Some sources round the issue size to ~₹51.92 Cr — minor discrepancy, likely fresh-issue vs total.)
- **Listing pop:** listed at ₹330 vs ₹165 issue price — ~100% listing gain ([ipopremium.in](https://www.ipopremium.in/view/ipo/282)).

### Migration from SME platform to NSE Main Board
- **The stock appears to have MIGRATED from NSE Emerge (SME) to the NSE Main Board.** This is strongly indicated by multiple market-data providers now classifying it as **"NSE Main Board"** rather than SME — Groww ([groww.in](https://groww.in/stocks/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd)), Investing.com ([in.investing.com](https://in.investing.com/equities/vinyas-innovative-tech)), and ValueResearch ([valueresearchonline.com](https://www.valueresearchonline.com/stocks/325037/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd/)), plus the plain NSE get-quote URL `/get-quote/equity/VINYAS` (SME symbols carry a "-SM"/"SM" suffix) ([nseindia.com](https://www.nseindia.com/get-quote/equity/VINYAS/Vinyas-Innovative-Technologies-Limited)).
- ⚠️ **UNVERIFIED — exact migration date.** I could not find an authoritative circular or news item stating the precise effective date of the main-board migration. NSE's revised SME→Main Board migration criteria took effect **May 1, 2025** ([business-standard.com](https://www.business-standard.com/markets/news/nse-raises-eligibility-criteria-for-direct-sme-migration-to-mainboard-125042401224_1.html)); migration would have occurred around or after that, but treat the date as unconfirmed pending an NSE circular / company filing.
- ⚠️ **Ticker note:** NSE symbol is **VINYAS** (ISIN INE0OLS01010). Some providers display a code **"VINV"** (Investing.com) — this is a provider-specific symbol, not the NSE ticker.

### Other historical milestones (from company site; treat as company-stated, semi-verified)
- **2011–2012:** Received "The Best Employer" award from the National Trust, Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Govt. of India ([vinyasit.com](https://www.vinyasit.com/company-overview/)).
- **2015:** Deepashree Narendra joined as VP – Corporate Affairs; **2018:** Sumukh Narendra joined as Executive Director ([vinyasit.com](https://www.vinyasit.com/company-overview/)).
- **Certifications:** ISO 9001:2015, **AS9100-D** (aerospace), ISO 13485:2016 (medical) — enabling entry into aerospace/defense and medical supply chains ([screener.in](https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/)).
- **FY2025 financials (reported):** revenue ~₹400.15 crore (+24.74% YoY); net profit ~₹19.42 crore (+26.56% YoY). Reported figures, single-source — verify against filings ([bitget.com profile](https://www.bitget.com/stock/nse-vinyas/what-is)).
- **Market cap** ~₹1,841 crore (small-cap), as of mid-2026 ([valueresearchonline.com](https://www.valueresearchonline.com/stocks/325037/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd/)).

### Data-quality notes
- chittorgarh.com, investorgain.com, ipoplatform.com, and ipocentral.in returned HTTP 403 and could not be fetched directly; their key figures were corroborated via search snippets and other fetchable sources (ipowatch, ipopremium, groww, screener, valueresearch).
- The two open items to confirm against primary filings (NSE circular / company investor-relations / RHP-DRHP): (1) **exact main-board migration date**, and (2) reconciling the **₹51.92 Cr vs ₹54.66 Cr** issue-size figures.
